data.title: SUI Crashes to Oversold Zone – Can Bulls Defend the Bottom?
data.description:

With sentiment deep in negative territory and price hovering near multi-month lows, traders are closely monitoring whether SUI can stabilize and reclaim critical levels in the coming days.

data.title: Bitcoin treasury firm Nakamoto transferred 1,003 BTC to Cobo as collateral for a previous $250 million funding round.
data.description: According to analyst Emmett Gallic's post on the X platform, Arkham monitoring data shows that Bitcoin treasury company Nakamoto (formerly Kindly MD) has transferred 1,003 BTC to Cobo as collateral for its previous $250 million convertible bond financing. This is the company's second additional BTC collateral this week. The initial offering price was set at Bitcoin's all-time high of $124,000. With the market downturn, margin pressure is evident, and the risk of excessive leverage needs to be closely monitored.

data.title: Planet Evening News
data.description: 1. Crypto ETFs saw a $2 billion outflow last week, the largest weekly outflow since February; 2. Wang Chun, co-founder of F2Pool: Currently holding and observing; 3. Bitwise CEO: Over $40 million in ETF inflows, crypto assets are cheap enough, will seize the opportunity to buy; 4. Delphi Digital: Institutional crypto demand has decreased by 90% from the August peak of $5.5 billion, capital is in a wait-and-see state; 5. Data: This week, 23 projects raised a total of $1.41 billion, Kalshi raised $1 billion at a valuation of $11 billion; 6. He Yi retweeted CZ's 2020 tweet that "BTC will plummet from $101,000 to $85,000" and remarked: Time flies; 7. Tether USDT's related gold reserves reached $12.9 billion, an increase of $7.6 billion in 9 months; 8. CryptoQuant CEO: It is too early to assert that US institutional investor confidence has fully recovered.

data.title: Hong Kong man sentenced to prison for role in attack to steal crypto
data.description:
A Hong Kong national has pleaded guilty to an elaborate attack involving breaking and entering, unlawful confinement, and sexual assault in an attempt to steal digital assets. As a result of the plea, the suspect, who was part of the gang that carried out the act, has been sentenced to seven years in prison. According […]

data.title: Bitcoin Collateral Increased Amid Market Pressure
data.description: According to PANews, analyst Emmett Gallic shared on the X platform that Arkham monitoring data reveals Nakamoto, formerly known as Kindly MD, has transferred 1,003 BTC to Cobo. This transfer serves as collateral for its previous $250 million convertible bond financing. This marks the company's second BTC collateral addition this week. The issuance price for this transaction was set at Bitcoin's all-time high of $124,000. As the market declines, the pressure on margins is evident, highlighting the need to closely monitor the risks associated with excessive leverage.

data.title: Chainlink, Solana, Wormhole, Drift, and Jito Lead Development Activity Across Solana’s Expanding Ecosystem
data.description:
Fresh data from Santiment shows a surge in development across the Solana ecosystem, with several major projects strengthening their technical foundations despite broader market volatility. The analytics platform ranked the top ten assets associated with Solana based on 30-day developer activity, revealing strong momentum among both core infrastructure providers and fast-growing Solana-native protocols.

data.title: An independent miner successfully packaged block 924569 with only 1.2 TH/s of hashrate, capturing 3.146 BTC in profit.
data.description: According to Cointelegraph, an independent Bitcoin miner successfully packaged block 924569 with a hash rate of only 1.2 TH/s, earning a block reward of 3.146 BTC, worth approximately $266,000. Based on the miner's reported hash rate, this probability is approximately one in 1.2 million per day.

data.title: Hong Kong Regulator Warns Against Unlicensed Virtual Asset Platform
data.description: According to PANews, the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) has issued a warning to the public regarding a virtual asset trading platform named 'AmazingTech.' The platform, which claims to operate locally in Hong Kong, has been added to the SFC's list of suspicious virtual asset trading platforms due to its alleged unlicensed activities.

data.description:
A former LAPD officer was denied bail on Friday after appearing in court on allegations of kidnapping and attempted theft of $350,000 in digital assets.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/former-lapd-officer-raid-steal-350000-crypto/

data.title: Solana Flashes Reversal Signals as Wallet Creation Accelerates
data.description:
Solana has spent most of November under heavy pressure, but new data suggests the trend may be shifting. The asset is trading around $127, extending a multi-week decline that pushed SOL nearly 50% below its September peak. Yet, despite the drawdown, fresh on-chain metrics and short-term forecasts indicate early signs of improving momentum
source: https://www.ethnews.com/solana-flashes-reversal-signals-as-wallet-creation-accelerates/

data.title: Ethereum co-founder Joseph: There is a lag in the disclosure of information regarding listed companies' increases in cryptocurrency holdings; the market needs to adjust and adapt.
data.description: Ethereum co-founder Joseph Lubin stated in an article on the X platform that recent filings show institutions have been steadily increasing their positions in SharpLink. While the stock and crypto markets are adjusting to the current macroeconomic environment, ownership data indicates growing institutional confidence. Delayed disclosures make this easily overlooked, which is another reason why tokenized stocks will eventually offer clearer, real-time transparency.

data.title: Solo Bitcoin Miner Achieves Rare Success with Minimal Computational Power
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, a solo Bitcoin miner achieved a remarkable feat on Friday by earning 3.146 BTC, valued at approximately $266,000, after solving block 924,569. This was accomplished with a significantly lower computational power than typically required for such a reward. The miner, reportedly using a hobby-grade machine, managed this success with a hash rate of about 1.2 terahashes per second (TH/s), a stark contrast to the industrial-scale operations that usually operate at exahashes per second. 

CKpool creator Con Kolivas shared the news on X, highlighting the miner's extraordinary luck and the improbability of the event. Kolivas estimated the odds of this occurrence at about 1.2 million to one per day, given the miner's hash rate. The miner's reward comprised 3.125 Bitcoin (BTC) from the block subsidy and an additional 0.021 BTC from transaction fees, totaling just over 3.146 BTC, as confirmed by onchain data.

Despite the dominance of industrial mining operations, 2025 has proven to be a notable year for solo miners. Mempool Space reports that 13 solo-mined blocks have been discovered through CKpool this year, averaging slightly more than one per month. In a similar instance last month, a solo Bitcoin miner earned a $347,455 reward after independently solving block 920,440, receiving 3.125 BTC plus fees. Earlier in July, another miner with just 2.3 petahashes of power claimed a full block reward, with similar successes recorded in June, March, and February.

In the broader mining industry, major players are exploring new revenue streams beyond Bitcoin mining, particularly following the latest halving that has tightened profit margins. CleanSpark has initiated a shift towards AI-focused data center infrastructure, a move that resulted in a 13% increase in its stock after the expansion was announced in October. Similarly, TeraWulf plans to raise $500 million through a convertible note offering to finance the construction of a new data center campus in Abernathy, Texas. These strategic pivots indicate a growing trend among Bitcoin miners to diversify their operations and adapt to changing market conditions.
